perItemFindings.label: S1
perItemFindings.one_line: The trace incorrectly says Causalean has no non-Gaussian source/mixing artifact: `Causalean.Discovery.LiNGAM.Kurtosis.cross_fourth_cumulant_eq_sum` models independent centered finite-fourth-moment sources and linear mixtures. It is not a total fit for overcomplete all-order LvLiNGAM, but the bypass must explicitly survey and reject it on that basis.

perItemFindings.label: oeq:real-exceptional-atlas
perItemFindings.one_line: The core node is the derived finite-atlas conclusion, but its planned `lean_kind:"assumption"` is only the conjunction of external I-3 QE/CAD and I-4 truncated-Hamburger interfaces; those facts do not themselves realize the core conclusion. Keep I-3/I-4 as separate external assumptions and plan the atlas as their conditional derived theorem.

perItemFindings.label: thm:generic-apolar-arrow-recovery / oeq:generic-separation / oeq:generic-exceptional-locus / oeq:minimal-real-information-order
perItemFindings.one_line: I-1 and I-2 are named research-scale external prerequisites only in notes, not explicit assumed interfaces in the consuming declarations' `hyps`; calling this a P4 constraint does not thread the conditional dependencies.

verbatim_critique: Update S1's substrate survey with the existing LiNGAM kurtosis module and record its concrete abstraction mismatch. Split I-3 and I-4 into separate external interface assumptions, derive the real-atlas conclusion conditionally from them, and represent I-1/I-2 as explicit external assumptions threaded through every deferred theorem that uses them; do not use a core-derived theorem node as a bare proxy for its substrate.
